channel.title: Data, Code and Visualization
channel.description: Notes and experiments about data, statistics, R, code, and visualization.

item.title: Many models, many years later
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: The talk that changed how I write R.

item.title: My favorite ggplot2 packages with examples
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: The idea is to show some packages and options that I use on a daily basis, and leave code with annotations and defaults that I normally use. We will go through the packages one by one with examples, but these can be grouped into the following categories.

item.title: Visualización en el Análisis de Datos
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: De vez en cuando tengo que hacer clases de visualización o análisis de datos, enseñar ggplot2 o dplyr, y en dichas ocaciones especiales siempre retomo un ya muy viejo ejemplo con datos del metro de Santiago de Chile en el cual intento motivar el hecho de que a través de distintas visualización uno va aprediendo de los datos.

item.title: Simulating and watching autoregressive processes
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: Always love time series when I studied statistics. This is a post to honor the time series (no {shiny} required).

item.title: Regression, ANOVA, t-test are related…
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: Source: https://stats.stackexchange.com/questions/59047/how-are-regression-the-t-test-and-the-anova-all-versions-of-the-general-linear

item.title: valueBoxes and Sparklines
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: The value boxes are a good way to get to the point quickly in a dashboard showing the relevant value with a big font and eye-catching color. But sometimes it would be better show that relevant number with a related text, sparkline or information to give context of that value, or just to compare it with the previoues ones. In this post we’ll show how to add sparklines to valueBox in shinydashboard and other related packages.

item.title: The tale of two charts combined
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: Post updated on Jul 20, 2026

item.title: #30diasdegraficos Parte 3
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: La tercera parte (y última?) Queda un buen trecho por recorrer y quedan características, opciones y series que nos falta ver, revisar y aprender.

item.title: #30diasdegraficos Parte 2
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: Seguiremos utilizando los mismos paquetes que la parte anterior.

item.title: #30diasdegraficos Parte 1
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: Agradecer por la iniciativa a la comunidad de R4DS_es <3:

item.title: #GOT Animating the Shifting of Affiliations
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: Why use GOT data? Because I was participating in #datosdemiercoles which is the spanish version of #tidytuesday. So the data is given and the purpouse is to learn new packages using that data and share with the community, you know this already, right?

item.title: When charts are integrated in the web page
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: I was searching for a CDN at jsdelivr.com and I noticed a chart showing download per days:

item.title: Using tooltips in unexpected ways
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: Highcharts tooltips are one of most underated features. In the tooltips you can easily render html, that means we can put images, tables even charts. So the possibilities are almost limitless.

item.title: Giving a Thematic Touch to your Interactive Chart
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: Sometimes you need to express yourself.

item.title: Thematic Interactive Map
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: Last week all the #rstatsosphere see a beautiful swiss map from Timo Grossenbacher using just ggplot2. Now is the turn of highcharter because all can be better with a bit of interactivity.

item.title: Interactive and Styled Middle Earth Map
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: Post updated on Oct 01, 2024

item.title: Replicating NYT Weather App
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: So much time since my last post so I want to post something, no matter what it is, but I hope this will be somehow helpful.

item.title: Adding motion to choropleths
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: Time ago hrbrmstr show how to replicate a visualization made by New York Times with R.

item.title: How to: Weather Radials
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: I was surfing by the deep seas of the web and I found the Brice Pierre de la Briere’s blocks and I saw the weather radials which originally are a poster collection. Brice uses D3 and he used D3 very well and I love D3 but I’m in a rookie level to do something like him. D3 is not for everybody and surely not for me, I would love to lear more but family, work and R has priority over D3 so how can I do something like that. Well… We have R & highcharter. So let’s try.

item.title: Visualizing Chess Data With ggplot
item.dc:creator: Joshua Kunst Fuentes
item.description: There are nice visualizations from chess data: piece movement, piece survaviliy, square usage by player, etc. Sadly not always the authors shows the code/data for replicate the final result. So I wrote some code to show how to do some this great visualizations entirely in R. Just for fun.
